Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 23 points with 6 lead changes and 17 ties. The game saw 46 major win probability swings, and the losing team's chances peaked at 67%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q3, 11:32, McCollum (ATL): McCollum P.FOUL (P2.T1) (M.Dagher). It swung the win probability by +16.4 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q3, 10:16, Kuzma (MIL): Kuzma Traveling Turnover (P4.T15). It swung the win probability by -26.9 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 11, away team 7.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q2, 11:10: McCollum 24' 3PT Step Back Jump Shot (9 PTS) (Kispert 1 AST). That single play moved ATL's chance of winning from 54% to 66%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went ATL's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than McCollum of ATL. Across 37 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 56 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q1, 9:41, with MIL down 5 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 35%. In other words: possible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q2, 6:06, with the game tilting ATL's way. The play in the middle of it: Alexander-Walker 25' 3PT Pullup Jump Shot (11 PTS) (Johnson 5 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
